item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so damn creative, definitely one of my favorite items from Wondertainment.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Posting here for clarity.

The image has been removed due to the author not releasing their sculptures under creative commons. To avoid any futures issues arising from SCP related content, the Licensing team has decided to remove this image to respect the original creator's wishes.

Anyone who wishes to continue viewing the Dragon Snails are free to go onto Erin Metcalf's website to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering it's not killing anybody for the lulz I get the feeling AWCY? wouldn't make it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm… it says that they're an artificial species… the Real Life image is of someone's artwork… Why not have them be the work of Are We Cool Yet? instead of Dr. Wondertainment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think that no sane person would name a fire breathing snail

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely needs some grammar and clinical tone edits, but otherwise, this is a great SCP that holds up well with the rest of Wondertainment's more recent stuff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone should go through and fix grammar. Aside from that, I NEED ONE. If these were real I would have one already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because that wouldn't be a good thing to do, and in this case we're only using the image with the permission of the creator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The signings in the image where not cropped out. Just realized it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I…I want…I want SO MANY OF THESE. I want ALL OF THE DRAGON SNAILS. Where's Dr. Wondertainment when you need her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the honor duel bit at the end got me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I call a Gunkwyvern >:U Gimme my pet chub

But seriously, I honestly want one of these.

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Actually, WestyDude, TerashimaRei's post here looks fine to me (especially given that the article is dragonsnails and we get plenty of similar comments on other cute SCP articles, plus it's just the one comment).

Please refrain from telling other users how to act. If you feel that someone is spamming, let a staff member know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Note: Not staff

This type of post is edging onto the territory of spamming. Try refraining of making random posts on the forums in the future. It would make the staff a lot happier.

Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 10/10 would buy a dragonsnail.

item.title: Re: Attribution
item.content:encoded: The link to the image location is dead. The image seems to be only available on DeviantArt: http://eirewolfcreations.deviantart.com/art/DragonSnails-1st-Sight-Kinoko-132389179

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could upvote like five times because 'honor duels'

Guh, I want ALL the dragon snails.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorite SCPs.

Where can I order a pair of Glowdrakes? :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There so cute D: I want one…two….many! °_°

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want a Dragonsnail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited it to link to her DeviantArt gallery. Link can be restored once domain is up again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The link in the picture caption appears to have gone dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DRAGONSNAILS, DRAGONSNAILS, IT'S ALMOST TIME FOR DRAGONSNAILS!

My 343, I'm sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it's severely divorced from any known evolutionary tree, it's more intelligent than they think it should be, Wondertainment isn't inclined to make mundane things, and because of the [DATA EXPUNGED] that lets it ignite its methane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so how is this anomalous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dragon Snails. Awesome.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noting here that the owner of the Dragonsnails image, Erin Metcalf, has stated that she does not want the picture to be licensed under creative commons.

As such, anyone intending to use the image must contact her and obtain her permission prior to using the image for anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really like the edit in the photo box to give the credit there. Real spiffy, and a creative way to do the atrribution. ^_^

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Please don't reply to staff posts if you're not staff. :)

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded:
As far as I can see, it's still the cropped picture that's up. Maybe I'm not seeing it, or my browser's stubbornly caching the image, though.

I'm in the market for a pair of glasses. Had to enlarge the page though. That's one seriously unobtrusive watermark. ;)

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Image is up again, with proper watermark. Artist has been contacted and has responded.

item.title: Attribution
item.content:encoded: Picture credit goes to Erin Metcalf. Website: http://www.EirewolfCreations.com. Image location: http://www.eirewolfcreations.com/?page=gallery&gallery_id=1&piece_id=110&picture_id=8

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Due to the attribution issues mentioned directly upthread, the image for this page has been temporarily removed. We'll keep y'all updated if anything new happens with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good on ya, Zyn.

I wish the best here for resolution of the issue. I'm still relatively new, but these things are seriously *squee*-worthy and I'd love to be able to keep the image. It's just too perfect! I'm guessing the image inspired the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got this.

Edit: Okay, the artist has been messaged. I'll talk specifics with them when they reply.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, I just found it.

The artist here.

The actual work here… Oh, god… uh, guys, you should read those comments. The artist knows about the article… Should someone message him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to repeat the question posed a few comments back… Where did the picture come from?

Also, I want one. And I don't think it's a bad thing for someone to think that. Heck, I want the best tothbrush ever. Brushing teeth is annoying sometimes. lol Doesn't make it a bad SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gah! I know this is an old post, but this is almost exactly what I was going to comment!

I honestly upvoted halfway through the article, then saw the "honor duels" line and wished I could upvote again. That made me lawl. Hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you sir, for lighting up my day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really cute, I almost want one.

I wish there was a picture for the Gunkwyvern.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just another scip
Hmm, this article is okay
Heh, they seem actually pretty cute
Wondertainment, JUST TAKE MY MONEY!

E: What, no honor duels?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YMMV, of course, but to me they're not just too… cute. Even with the fire-breathing, they don't have enough of the "Oh god what thinks this is a good idea to give to a child" vibe like most of the other Wondertainment stuff does for me. If this had a bit more of the not-quite-getting-it vibe, I'd like it more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We can't have -everything- here be grimdark and terrible. The Foundation ensures anomalous objects are kept out of the public eye, and if need be, to protect the public from them. Not everything that should-not-be is going to be a horrifying inter-dimensional, omni-murdering banana peel from beyond time. Sometimes you get the super-hugging orange blob, the eyepods, the rabbit that eats itself. All of them should-not-be. They're dangerous, for a given definition of the word, but they aren't malicious. The dragon-snails are dangerous, but they aren't malicious. Hell, Wondertainment isn't trying to be dangerous, he just wants to make people happy. That he does this in ways that -are- sometimes terrifying is what makes him one of the Foundation's 'most-wanted'. Wondertainment obviously doesn't know better, which lends some tragedy to him. He's not a bad person…thing..whatever he might be. He just seems to think we can survive alot more than we can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted, sorry. They're cute, but IMHO the reaction to an SCP, even a Wondertainment one, should not be 'ooh I want one!'.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Just discovered this, and I love it.
I demand a free Dragon-Snail now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do these have a warning label? I thought discovering what they did was supposed to be fun for the whole family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd almost up-vote on "honor duels" alone, but this is pretty spiffy otherwise as well.

If you're still looking for a hidden down-side, why not steal from Terry Pratchett? Anything with a fuel-producing digestive system and built-in flame-thrower is going to explode occasionally. These could be hand-grenades waiting to go off. A problem aggravated in groups, since the little fire-breaths wouldn't be much danger directly but could easily be enough to set off a second specimen, and the explosion could set off all the rest of the pack.

Just an idea, feel free to ignore, since it's good-enough as is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Want. Want. Want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how the article takes the idea of tiny pet dragons (which WOULD be too cool for Wondertainment) and then makes them snails. Pure awesome, upvoting.

I also like the sneaky note about how by playing with these, you waive rights to any protest, including boycotts. In other words, it's illegal to not buy more.

Oh, and I WANT THOSE TEST LOGS!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's the perfect "breather" (no pun intended)—believable, with just the right amount of humor. And very cool picture! Where did you come across that? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like these. I think that idea about having them be voracious eaters was pretty good, like consuming several times their body weight in lettuce a day. I like the idea of a tiny adorable invasive species that breathes fire.

That said, this article is good and you should feel good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wishing I could upvote twice for "honor duels."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll say this: if it wasn't Wondertainment, this would be pretty stupid. But the note at the end really makes it. That said, there are still some weird turns of phrase in the description. I'm slightly drunk at the moment, so I can't be more helpful than that, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god I want to write that now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe if you feed it something you DO want, they get enraged?
Just a thought…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, they'd definitely be an invasive species. Maybe some addendum of pet dragonsnails that were lost/thrown away by their owners is at order. Still, I want some of them so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. I immensely dislike this reasoning. This sort of thing would be kept on a low-hazard bio-containment site, most likely in several large terraria. For one , a 5m tall enclosure is friggin' impractical when you need to manipulate with them… for two ,I can't imagine any reason the Foundation would have pre-built 125m^3 cubes lying around…
And yes, there are standard types of enclosure such as the beloved Standard Humanoid Containment cell, however they are more sensibly dimensioned.

2. Good addition. I'm liking them more and more. And tacking on cheap danger wouldn't work well for this either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry Voct, I had to re-add the bit about breeding your own. That was a big part of the idea for me, that you could interbreed the types. Presumably this is just the "starter pack" and Wondertainment would make loads more types of 'snail.

Thanks for removing "nice" though. That was quite nice of you.

EDIT: In the future could you please ask me before changing stuff about the page content?
